visible_observations: An upright ovoid creature stands on two short hoof-like legs, enclosed by overlapping pale carapace plates separated by dark fibrous seams.
The front opens around a bound cocoon-like inner body with a vertical toothed maw, while a second tapered structure above it carries a narrow purple slit.
Cracks, blood-dark seams, corded tissue, and irregular plate edges keep the body tactile and worn rather than clean or ornamental.
strengths: The nested shell, exposed central captive or organ, double vertical apertures, and compressed legs create a singular nonhuman body plan.
Horror is embodied through enclosure, binding, teeth, and incompatible anatomical layers rather than detached effects.
defects: The nearly symmetrical upright presentation is static and defensive; no limb, weight shift, or attack line visibly demonstrates how the creature threatens or fights.
The central bound body can read as a victim carried inside a protective seed pod, which competes with the read of the whole form as one hostile actor.
uncertainties: Unaided inspection cannot determine whether the purple slit, toothed opening, or inner cocoon is the controlling intelligence.
The movement method and gameplay action are not visually certain beyond slow armored advance.

visible_observations: Three black silhouettes explore a tall closed blade-like pod on two legs, a low wide opened shell supported by multiple articulated legs, and an upright oval shell opened around a central cocoon on two legs.
The candidates materially vary height, locomotion, negative space, shell opening, and how much internal anatomy is exposed.
strengths: Candidate A is a severe vertical spike, candidate B is an arachnid-like low mass, and candidate C is a contained upright reliquary; the alternatives are genuinely distinct body plans.
Candidate C retains both the leaf-armored outline and a readable central void that separates the inner body from the shell.
defects: Candidate A is so closed that it reads primarily as a seed or architectural fin, while candidate C's paired legs and smooth oval mass reduce kinetic threat.
Candidate B supplies the clearest predatory locomotion but its dense shell hides the internal mouth-and-cocoon identity.
uncertainties: The sheet does not establish whether any shell parts articulate during movement or attack.

visible_observations: The enlargement contains no humanoid face: a narrow purple slit sits in a tapered upper plate above a wrapped inner body whose vertical opening is lined with nested teeth.
Rigid pale plates crowd both apertures while dark cords cross the inner body, producing a restrained, compressed, and invasive relationship between shell and core.
strengths: The absence of a conventional face is a coherent nonhuman mechanism, and the slit-over-maw arrangement creates a watchful upper sensor paired with feeding anatomy.
Binding cords and enclosing plates make confinement or compulsion physically visible.
defects: Neither aperture has directional focus, asymmetrical tension, or a visible target, so the image communicates alien anatomy more clearly than a current psychological state.
The wrapped inner form still risks reading as prey or offspring rather than the active face of the creature.
uncertainties: It is unclear which aperture perceives the viewer and whether the visible relationship is predation, gestation, imprisonment, or symbiosis.

visible_observations: At native size, the pale oval shell, dark central cleft, repeated diagonal plates, and two short legs remain legible.
The purple slit, inner bindings, tooth structure, blood-dark seams, and distinction between the two apertures disappear into the narrow dark center.
strengths: The closed leaf or pinecone outline is immediately distinct and the light shell separates cleanly from the dark background.
The central opening survives as a simple high-contrast structural cue.
defects: The native read is closer to a stationary seed pod than an active hostile creature because the small legs and internal maw carry little visual weight.
Threat, psychology, and the bound-inner-body concept do not survive at this scale.
uncertainties: The image alone does not establish whether players would read an armored creature, plant pod, egg, or environmental object during gameplay.
